title: excoecaria biglandulosa var. serrata
intro: Excoecaria biglandulosa var. serrata, also known as the 'Giant Yellow Oleander', is a variant of the Excoecaria biglandulosa plant, native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Excoecaria biglandulosa var. serrata include treating fever, skin conditions, and digestive issues, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and insect bites. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and the plant's sap has been used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine.
